text: Testing plays a vital role in modern software development. Unit tests check individual functions or components, ensuring they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests then verify that different parts of a system communicate and function well together. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write a test before coding the actual feature. This approach ensures code meets requirements from the start. While writing tests can be time-consuming, they bring confidence to the development process. When you change or add code, passing tests tell you that nothing has broken. This safety net allows teams to improve their software faster, with fewer bugs and greater reliability. Overall, testing turns guesswork into evidence-based quality.
